name: Chairman Semi-Mono
classification_notes: Part of the Chairman Mono System, this is a semi-monospaced variant sitting between the fully monospaced Chairman Mono and the proportional Chairman Text and Display families, designed for coding-adjacent or editorial contexts where rhythm matters.
summary: A semi-monospaced member of Typotheque's Chairman Mono System, available in five weights with slanted companions and a variable font option.
description: Part of the broader Chairman Mono System alongside the proportional Text and Display cuts and the fully monospaced Chairman Mono, this semi-mono variant occupies a middle ground suited to interfaces and editorial contexts that benefit from consistent rhythm without strict fixed-width spacing. Five weights run from Light to Bold, each paired with a slanted style, and the family is also available as a variable font. The system's city-named styles hint at a wide intended usage across international editorial and digital environments.
foundry_blurb: Chairman Semi-Mono is part of Typotheque's Chairman Mono System, a comprehensive type family that spans fully monospaced, semi-monospaced, text, and display variants. The semi-mono subfamily offers five weights from Light to Bold with slanted counterparts, and is also available as a variable font.

name: Chairman Text
classification_notes: Chairman Text is the text-optimised cut of the Chairman system, designed for smaller sizes with a serif construction suited to extended reading. Its optical sizing alongside Display and Mono subfamilies places it within a structured transitional serif system.
summary: The text-optimised cut of Typotheque's Chairman system, covering five weights with italics and a variable font option for extended reading work.
description: Chairman Text is one of four subfamilies within the Chairman type system, sitting alongside Chairman Display, Chairman Mono and Chairman Semi-Mono. The text cut runs from Light to Bold with matching italics, calibrated for smaller sizes and continuous reading rather than headlines. A variable font version is available, and the broader system's inclusion of mono and semi-mono variants makes it well-suited for editorial or interface projects requiring typographic consistency across contexts.
foundry_blurb: Chairman Text is part of the Chairman System, which comprises Text, Display, Mono and Semi-Mono subfamilies. The Text family offers five weights from Light to Bold, each with italics, as well as a variable font, designed for use at reading sizes within a coherent multi-style typographic system.

name: Chairman Mono
classification_notes: Chairman Mono is explicitly a monospaced typeface with a semi-mono variant and variable font, designed for both code and editorial use across multiple optical sizes.
summary: A monospaced type system from Typotheque spanning four subfamilies, from strict mono through semi-mono to text and display opticals, with a variable font companion.
description: A thoroughly considered monospaced system with five sub-families covering Chairman Mono, Chairman Semi-Mono, Chairman Text and Chairman Display, plus a variable font. Weights run from Thin to Black depending on the subfamily, with slanted or italic companions throughout. The range of optical sizes makes it suited for everything from code and data interfaces to editorial headlines.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Chairman Mono as a complete type system built around a monospaced core, offering four distinct sub-families including a semi-mono variant, optical sizes optimised for text and display settings, slanted styles, and a variable font, with weights named after cities across the globe.

name: Chairman Display
classification_notes: Chairman Display sits within a broad sans-serif system with humanist proportions, featuring optical size variants (Display, Text, Mono, Semi-Mono) and a variable text subfamily, suggesting a text-oriented humanist approach rather than geometric or grotesque.
summary: A large type system by Zoran Pavlović and Matko Meštrović spanning Display, Text, Mono and Semi-Mono subfamilies across eight weights with italics.
description: An expansive humanist sans system from Typotheque covering four distinct subfamilies: Chairman Display, Chairman Text, Chairman Mono and Chairman Semi-Mono, each tuned for its intended context. Display and Text cuts share eight weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, while the Mono and Semi-Mono variants offer slanted rather than true italics. A variable Text subfamily adds further flexibility for screen and editorial use across a wide weight range.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Chairman as a comprehensive type system comprising Display, Text, Mono and Semi-Mono subfamilies, designed by Zoran Pavlović and Matko Meštrović. The system spans eight weights from Thin to Black with italic companions in the Display and Text cuts, and slanted variants in the Mono and Semi-Mono families, with a variable Text subfamily also available.

name: Terrassa Gradient
classification_notes: Terrassa Gradient is a variable display typeface built from modular, monolinear letterforms derived from architectural drafting conventions. The stepped weight transitions and depth-effect layering place it firmly in the display category, with geometric construction as its primary formal logic.
summary: A variable display typeface by Nikola Djurek that uses stepped weight transitions to simulate depth and motion, drawn from the monolinear lettering of architectural plans.
description: Part of the three-member Terrassa system, Terrassa Gradient applies five variable axes to produce the illusion of depth and movement through graduated stroke weights. The design traces its formal logic to technical drawing conventions, building letterforms from modular geometry rather than calligraphic tradition. It ships as a variable font specifically because the five axes would generate over 10,000 static variants, and supports Latin, Cyrillic and Greek scripts with an extensive language list.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Terrassa Gradient as one of three collections within the Terrassa type family, alongside Terrassa and Terrassa Display. Where Terrassa provides the foundational modular letterforms, Terrassa Gradient introduces stepped weight transitions to create the effect of depth and motion. All three collections draw on monolinear lettering from architectural plans and technical drawings, and Terrassa Gradient is delivered exclusively as a variable font because its five axes would otherwise produce more than 10,000 static variations.

name: Terrassa Display
classification_notes: Terrassa Display is a modular geometric display typeface with variable layered outlines and architectural repetition, taking clear cues from technical drawing and engineering lettering rather than any historical type revival.
summary: A modular variable display typeface by Nikola Djurek, built around architectural drafting geometry with layered outlines and a proportional weight-scaling system.
description: Part of a three-member system alongside Terrassa and Terrassa Gradient, the Display variant adds layered outlines and architectural repetition to the family's core monolinear geometry. Five variable axes replace what would otherwise be over 10,000 static instances, allowing fine control over stroke weight, layer depth and spatial rhythm. The proportional weight calculator, drawn from drafting practice, lets designers keep stroke widths consistent as type scales, preserving optical clarity across sizes.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Terrassa Display as a modular display typeface combining geometric precision with architectural expressiveness, inspired by monolinear lettering from technical drawings and architectural plans. Together with Terrassa and Terrassa Gradient, it forms a flexible system for expressive typographic design. The Display variant is delivered as a variable font with five axes, which Typotheque note would otherwise produce over 10,000 static variations.

name: Terrassa
classification_notes: Terrassa is a geometric sans-serif with an unusual optical-size weighting system where stroke width is calibrated to point size rather than conventional weight steps. The Tight and standard widths, plus Display and Gradient subfamilies, confirm a broad-ranging geometric sans system.
summary: A geometric sans system built around a weight calculator that maintains consistent stroke widths relative to point size, distributed across named styles tied to world cities.
description: Terrassa organises its weights not by conventional naming conventions alone but through a mathematically linked system where stroke width stays optically consistent as point size changes, allowing precise cross-size typesetting. Styles are named after cities from Amsterdam to Luxembourg, spanning Hairline to Medium and including Tight width variants alongside standard. Three subfamilies extend the system: Terrassa for text, Terrassa Display for large settings and Terrassa Gradient for decorative or experimental use.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Terrassa as a type system with three subfamilies: Terrassa, Terrassa Display and Terrassa Gradient. Its weight calculator is a central feature, designed so that stroke widths remain consistent regardless of the point size at which the type is set, with each style named after a world city.

name: Pristine
classification_notes: The name, weight range and Typotheque's general output suggest a refined serif design; the clean, upright presentation implied by 'Pristine' and the foundry's tradition of carefully crafted text faces point toward a transitional or classical serif subcategory.
summary: A four-weight serif family from Typotheque with a variable font option, covering Light through Bold for text and editorial use.
description: Available in four static weights from Light to Bold alongside a variable font, Pristine is a serif family designed for text setting across a range of sizes. The Latin character set includes swash alternates, suggesting attention to typographic detail beyond purely functional text use. Typotheque's foundry background ensures solid technical construction and broad language support within the Latin script.
foundry_blurb: Pristine is offered by Typotheque in Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights, with a variable font version also available. The family includes a full Latin glyph set with swash character variants.

name: Zed Icons
classification_notes: An icon and symbol font rather than a text typeface; classified under display as the closest applicable category given its purely pictographic, non-alphabetic nature.
summary: A variable icon font of 2000 symbols spanning accessibility, wayfinding, UI and weather, designed to pair with the Zed text and display families.
description: Released in 2025 by Ondrej Jób and engineered by Rafał Buchner, Zed Icons offers three adjustable variable axes covering weight, optical size and roundness, enabling hundreds of distinct style combinations from a single font file. The 2000 icons span eight categories including accessibility, wayfinding, transportation and UI elements, with particular care given to symbols communicating disability and priority access. Where variable font support is unavailable, Typotheque provides tools to generate static variants directly from a user's online account.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Zed Icons as designed to aid communication across language barriers and cultural boundaries, making information accessible to the general public and to users with cognitive disabilities. The three variable axes for weight, optical size and roundness allow hundreds of unique design styles, and the collection of 2000 icons covers accessibility, wayfinding, objects, symbols, transportation, weather, UI elements and warnings. It won a Gold European Design Award in 2024 and TDC Typographic Excellence in 2025.

name: Zed Text Semi-Wide

The Text cuts are optimised for body setting, with the Semi-Wide variant offering a slightly expanded proportion.
summary: Part of Typotheque's expansive Zed system, this semi-wide text cut is designed for sustained reading across editorial and interface contexts.
description: Zed Text Semi-Wide sits within one of the most structurally extensive type systems available, spanning compressed to extra-wide proportions across Display, Round and Text optical categories. The Text subfamily is tuned for body copy, with the Semi-Wide variant offering slightly broader letterforms suited to more open settings. Nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with a slanted companion, give it considerable range across print and screen applications.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Zed Text Semi-Wide as part of the Zed system, a vast geometric sans family covering multiple widths from Extra-Compressed to Extra-Wide, available in Display, Round and Text optical variants, along with an Icons subfamily and a variable font option.

Each style in the Round subfamily features softened, rounded terminals that distinguish it from the sharper Display cuts, while the Wide proportion pushes letterforms into open, spacious territory suited to headlines and branding at large sizes. Individual styles are named after world cities, running from Hairline through to Black, each available in upright, slanted and backslanted versions.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque's Zed is a large type system built across multiple subfamilies including Display, Round, Text and Icons, with width variants ranging from Extra-Compressed to Extra-Wide and weights from Hairline to Black. Zed Round Wide is the wide proportion of the rounded subfamily, offered in upright, slanted and backslanted cuts across the full weight range, with individual styles each named after a different world city.

summary: A compressed width cut from Typotheque's October system, offering nine weights with italics as part of a coordinated family spanning three widths.
description: October Compressed sits at the narrow end of the October type system, which runs across Compressed, Regular and Condensed widths, each with nine weights from Hairline to Black and matching italics. A variable font edition is also available, giving continuous access across the width and weight axes. The system suits editorial and display contexts where consistent typographic voice is needed across tightly packed and open settings alike.
foundry_blurb: October Compressed is part of Typotheque's October system, a large sans-serif family available in three widths — Compressed, Regular and Condensed — each spanning nine weights from Hairline to Black with companion italics. The family is also offered as October Variable for fluid control across axes.

name: October
classification_notes: October presents as a contemporary grotesque sans-serif with a wide weight range and extended width variants, consistent with the grotesque tradition of neutral, systematic letterforms suited to editorial and branding applications.
summary: A systematic grotesque family from Typotheque spanning nine weights across standard, condensed and compressed widths, with a variable font option.
description: A thorough grotesque sans covering nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with matching italics, across three width variants: October, October Condensed and October Compressed. The full variable font version consolidates the family into a single file for flexible deployment. City names from Amsterdam to Reykjavík label each style, suggesting a cosmopolitan editorial sensibility.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present October as a comprehensive type system with nine weights and matching italics across standard, condensed and compressed widths, alongside a variable font. Each style is named after a world city, from Amsterdam (Hairline) to Reykjavík (Black Italic).

name: Francis Gradient
classification_notes: Francis Gradient is a display-oriented variant of the Francis family, a humanist sans-serif. The gradient system adds directional shading variants (Left, Right, Inside, Outside) as a typographic effect layer on top of the underlying humanist sans structure.
summary: A directional shading system built on the Francis humanist sans, offering four gradient variants for display and branding use.
description: Francis Gradient extends the Francis typeface family with four shading directions: Left, Right, Inside and Outside, each creating a distinct three-dimensional or illuminated effect across the letterforms. The system runs across five weights from Light to Heavy, all with matching italics, giving designers considerable range within the gradient treatment. Used in book covers, branding and identity work where typographic depth is required without resorting to manual effects.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Francis Gradient as a system of four directional gradient variants built on the Francis family, with named cuts including Left, Right, Inside and Outside. The system spans five weights with italics, and has been applied to projects including the Recording Academy logo and ShapeApp branding.

name: Francis
classification_notes: Francis presents as a humanist sans-serif with a gradient display variant, suggesting optical refinement and a broad typographic system. The warmth of its naming conventions and the range of weights support a humanist classification.
summary: A humanist sans-serif system from Typotheque with five weights, matching italics and a gradient display variant in four directional styles.
description: Francis is a humanist sans-serif organised into five weights from Light to Heavy, each with a corresponding italic. The family extends into Francis Gradient, which offers four optical treatments — Left, Right, Inside and Outside — for expressive display use. City-named specimen styles from Amsterdam to Jerusalem give a sense of the intended international reach of the Latin character set.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Francis as a type system spanning five weights with italics and a dedicated Gradient subfamily offering Left, Right, Inside and Outside variants for display applications, with full Latin glyph coverage including small capitals.

name: November Condensed
classification_notes: November Condensed is a condensed grotesque sans-serif, part of a larger system that includes standard and compressed widths across a broad weight range, consistent with the Typotheque neo-grotesque tradition.
summary: A condensed grotesque from Typotheque, part of the November system spanning three widths and nine weights with matching italics and a variable font.
description: November Condensed sits within a large type system that covers Condensed, standard and Compressed widths, each running from Hairline to Black with full italic counterparts. The family is also available as a variable font, giving designers fine control across the weight and width axes. Its neutral grotesque character makes it well-suited to editorial layouts, branding and display settings where economy of space is needed.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present November Condensed as part of the wider November system, which includes standard and Compressed variants alongside a variable font. The family spans nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic, and is shown in use across publications and cultural institutions.

name: November Compressed
classification_notes: November Compressed is part of the November System, a multi-width grotesque sans family. The compressed width sits alongside the standard and condensed cuts, sharing the same nine-weight grotesque structure.
summary: The compressed width variant of Typotheque's November System, a nine-weight grotesque family available in three widths and a variable version.
description: November Compressed is one of three width variants within the November System, sitting alongside the standard and condensed cuts. Nine weights run from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic, giving the system 54 styles across the non-variable widths. A variable font rounds out the offering, covering the full weight and width range in a single file.
foundry_blurb: November Compressed forms part of Typotheque's November System, a wide-ranging grotesque sans available in Compressed, Regular and Condensed widths, each spanning nine weights from Hairline to Black with italics throughout, plus a variable font version.

name: November
classification_notes: November is a contemporary grotesque sans-serif with a large weight range and extended width variants, following the conventions of European grotesque typeface families.
summary: A large grotesque system from Typotheque spanning nine weights across upright, condensed and compressed widths, with a variable font option.
description: November covers nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with matching italics, across three width variants: standard, Condensed and Compressed. The system totals 54 static styles alongside a variable font, giving considerable range for typographic hierarchies in editorial, branding and identity work. The weight extremes and width compression make it particularly suited to display and headline setting.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present November as a system with three sub-families — November, November Condensed and November Compressed — each available in nine weights with italics, plus a variable font covering the full range.

name: Zico Slab
classification_notes: Zico Slab is a slab serif forming part of the broader Zico system alongside a sans and condensed sans, sharing structural proportions across the family. The upright and italic cuts across seven weights confirm its classification as a slab serif.
summary: A slab serif component of the wider Zico superfamily, running seven weights with italics and coordinating with a sans, condensed sans and display subfamily.
description: Zico Slab sits within a tightly integrated type system that spans a slab, a sans, a condensed sans and a display family, all sharing common proportions and structural logic. Seven weights from Thin to Black are available with matching italics, giving broad range for editorial and corporate publishing work. The companion Zico Display adds Outline and Inline variants for headline use, rounding out a versatile multi-purpose system.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Zico Slab as part of the Zico system, a superfamily encompassing Zico Slab, Zico Sans, Zico Sans Condensed and Zico Display, with the slab running seven weights from Thin to Black, each with an italic counterpart.

name: Zico Display
classification_notes: Zico Display is a display-specific subfamily within the Zico system, offering decorative optical variants including Thin, Outline, Black and Inline cuts suited to large-scale headline use rather than text setting.
summary: The decorative display arm of the Zico type system, offering Thin, Outline, Black and Inline variants designed for large-scale headline and identity work.
description: Zico Display sits at the expressive end of the broader Zico family, which spans Zico Sans, Zico Sans Condensed and Zico Slab. The Display subfamily provides four stylistically distinct cuts: Thin, Outline, Black and Inline, each suited to poster and branding contexts where contrast and character at large sizes matter. The system has been applied to sports branding, as seen in the Salisol identity shown on the product page.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Zico Display as part of the wider Zico system, which includes Zico Sans, Zico Sans Condensed and Zico Slab. The Display subfamily offers four variants: Thin, Outline, Black and Inline, designed for display applications at large sizes.

name: Delvard Display
classification_notes: Delvard Display is a high-contrast serif intended for large-scale typesetting, with optical cuts and named subfamilies suggesting extreme stroke variation typical of display-oriented type design.
summary: A high-contrast display serif from Typotheque, offered in six named subfamilies and six weight-like cuts ranging from One through Fat.
description: Delvard Display is organised around six named subfamilies — Amsterdam, Bengaluru, Copenhagen, Damascus, Edinburgh and Fortaleza — each sold individually, with weights running from One to Fat. The naming convention suggests distinct stylistic variants rather than a conventional weight axis. Suited to poster, headline and editorial work where strong contrast and typographic personality are required.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque presents Delvard Display as a family of six subfamilies, each available to purchase separately, with cuts running from One through to Fat, covering the Latin script.

name: Typonine Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil typeface system with multiple weights and widths, designed for display use; the stencil construction defines its category, while the range of weights and condensed cuts give it extended utility across print and exhibition contexts.
summary: A stencil type system with four weights, condensed cuts and several sub-families including the italic-only Thema and the dual-variant Thema Moderato.
description: The system spans a core stencil family in Regular, Medium and Bold with matching condensed cuts, alongside Thema, which provides Light through Bold italics, and Thema Moderato, which offers A and B variants across four weights with and without stencil treatment. Each weight in the main family is named after a city, from Amsterdam to Fortaleza. Exhibition and catalogue work, as seen in the Terra Incognita catalogue, appears to be the intended territory.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Typonine Stencil as a broad system encompassing the main stencil family in regular and condensed widths across three weights, the italic sub-family Thema, and Thema Moderato with its paired A and B variants available both with and without stencil cuts.

name: Typonine Sans Condensed
classification_notes: The condensed companion to Typonine Sans, this is a humanist sans with a system architecture spanning multiple widths and weights, consistent with Typotheque's text-focused type families.
summary: The condensed width variant of Typonine Sans, offering four weights with italics for space-efficient text setting.
description: Part of the wider Typonine Sans system alongside the regular width and Hairline subfamilies, the condensed cut covers Light through Bold with matching italics. Individual styles are sold under city names, a convention Typotheque uses across several of its families. Latin character support is confirmed, and the system architecture suits editorial and information design where width economy matters.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Typonine Sans Condensed as part of the broader Typonine Sans system, which also includes the standard width family and a Hairline variant. Individual styles are named after cities and sold separately, with weights running from Light to Bold, each with a corresponding italic.

name: Typonine Sans
classification_notes: Typonine Sans presents clean, rational letterforms with a humanist warmth, organised into a system of standard and condensed widths across multiple weights, typical of contemporary humanist sans design.
summary: A structured sans-serif system with standard, condensed and hairline subfamilies, spanning four weights with matching italics.
description: Organised into three subfamilies, Typonine Sans covers standard and condensed widths plus a hairline cut, each running across Light, Regular, Medium and Bold with italics throughout. The system is well-suited to editorial and information design where width variation and tonal range are needed within a consistent voice. Named city editions are offered as individual weights for separate purchase.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Typonine Sans as a system comprising Typonine Sans, Typonine Sans Condensed and Typonine Hairline, each available across four weights with italics, offered individually by named city edition or as a complete family.

name: Typonine Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface with Latin coverage, offered in multiple weights with italics, straightforwardly classified as mono.
summary: A clean monospaced family from Typotheque spanning four weights with matching italics, covering extended Latin.
description: Typonine Mono runs from Light to Bold across four weights, each with a corresponding italic, giving it reasonable range for coding environments or tabular editorial work. The family covers Latin script and carries city names as style identifiers across its eight fonts. Typotheque's release keeps things functional and restrained without distinguishing features that set it apart from the wider monospaced category.
foundry_blurb: Typonine Mono is a monospaced family available in Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights, each with an italic companion. The eight fonts cover extended Latin glyphs and are available to buy directly from Typotheque.

name: Typonine Hairline
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a humanist sans serif, the Hairline is the extreme thin cut of the broader Typonine family, designed with warm, balanced proportions despite near-zero stroke contrast.
summary: A hairline-weight humanist sans by Nikola Djurek, designed exclusively for very large sizes where its near-zero stroke contrast still reads as warm and refined.
description: Part of Nikola Djurek's wider Typonine collection, this hairline cut sits at the extreme thin end of the family and is intended for exhibition settings, posters and large editorial headlines. Despite having no visible contrast between thick and thin strokes, the letterforms retain the warm, personal character of a humanist sans. Eight numeral styles are included, from proportional lining to circled inverted figures, with the option to set a custom default. Cyrillic was drawn by Ilya Ruderman, extending coverage across a substantial range of Latin and Cyrillic languages.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Typonine Sans Hairline as the thinnest font in the Typonine collection, a clean, low-contrast humanist sans serif intended for the largest sizes. They note that despite the absence of visible stroke contrast it remains refined, balanced, warm and personal, and recommend using it with generous space in exhibition settings, posters and large editorial pages. It forms part of a broader system that includes Sans, Condensed, Monospaced and Stencil variants, designed to be combined with the related serif Thema.

name: Tremolo Shadow
classification_notes: Tremolo Shadow is a display typeface system built for chromatic typography, with separate top and bottom layer styles that combine to produce three-dimensional shadow effects. Its eclectic character draws on cursive calligraphy, brush lettering and condensed blackletter, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A chromatic shadow layer system extracted from the broader Tremolo family, designed by Nikola Djurek for multi-colour typesetting with interlocking top and bottom styles.
description: Part of Typotheque's Tremolo collection, the Shadow system splits each letterform into discrete top and bottom layer fonts that register together to produce cast-shadow effects in two colours. The underlying design is narrow and eclectic, drawing on cursive calligraphy, brush lettering and condensed blackletter to produce a voice that shifts from austere to playful depending on context. Released in 2015 and recognised with a TDC Typographic Excellence award in 2016, it suits editorial headings, posters and any setting where chromatic type is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Tremolo as a collection of narrow and eclectic typefaces exploring new territories of type design, difficult to classify and containing elements of cursive calligraphy, brush lettering and condensed blackletter. The Shadow styles use separate top and bottom layer fonts intended for chromatic typography, allowing two-colour shadow effects through layering.

name: Tremolo Gradient
classification_notes: Tremolo Gradient is a layered display type system with top and bottom gradient layers designed to be stacked for chromatic gradient effects, placing it firmly in the display category with a specialised layering subcategory.
summary: A layered gradient display system from Typotheque, built from paired top and bottom layers that combine to produce smooth chromatic transitions across letterforms.
description: Part of the broader Tremolo type system, the Gradient subfamily provides six layers in three pairs (A, B and C), each split into a Top and Bottom component. Overlaying the paired layers produces gradient colour effects across the glyphs, suited to poster, exhibition and social media work. The system sits alongside Tremolo Display, Text, Stencil and Sans families, allowing designers to mix gradient and solid treatments within a single typographic family.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tremolo Gradient as part of the wider Tremolo system, which includes Display, Text, Italic, Stencil and Sans subfamilies. The Gradient layers are designed to be combined in pairs to create gradient colour effects, with three variant pairs (A, B and C) each split into Top and Bottom components for layered composition.

name: Tremolo
classification_notes: Tremolo is positioned as a display typeface with a layered system including stencil, shadow, and gradient variants, indicating a decorative display orientation. The presence of a companion sans subfamily suggests a broader display type system.
summary: A layered display type system from Typotheque comprising stencil, shadow and gradient variants alongside a companion sans family.
description: Tremolo is built as a modular system rather than a single typeface, with distinct subfamilies for stencil, shadow and gradient effects that can be overlaid or used independently. The companion Tremolo Sans spans five weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, providing a functional counterpart to the more expressive display cuts. The system is organised into named packages, suggesting a city-based licensing or bundle structure.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tremolo as a system of display typefaces incorporating standard, stencil, shadow and gradient variants, alongside a multi-weight sans companion. Packages are available individually or in combination, with separate options for text and display sizes.

name: Thema Moderato Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil variant of the Thema Moderato family, with interrupted strokes and bridged counters characteristic of stencil typefaces; categorised as display due to its specialised decorative and structural stencil construction.
summary: A stencil variant of the Thema Moderato family, available in two alternating glyph sets and four weights from Light to Bold.
description: Part of Typotheque's broader Thema system, this stencil cut offers two parallel glyph variants — A and B — across four weights, allowing typographers to mix interrupted and bridged letterforms for varied optical texture. The family connects to the wider Thema ecosystem, which includes Thema, Thema Moderato and the related Typonine Stencil with condensed options. Its modular structure suits editorial, wayfinding and print applications where stencil aesthetics are called for.
foundry_blurb: Thema Moderato Stencil is presented by Typotheque as part of an interconnected system that includes Thema Moderato, Thema and Typonine Stencil. The stencil family offers A and B glyph variants across Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights, each sold individually and named after cities from Amsterdam to Hong Kong.

name: Thema Moderato
classification_notes: Thema Moderato appears to be part of the Thema type system from Typotheque, a serif family with stencil variants and multiple optical sub-styles, consistent with a transitional or contemporary serif classification.
summary: Part of Typotheque's Thema system, offering two parallel serif sub-styles across four weights alongside stencil and condensed variants.
description: Thema Moderato is a serif type system from Typotheque offering two optical variants, labelled A and B, across four weights from Light to Bold. The system extends into stencil cuts via Thema Moderato Stencil, and connects to the broader Thema family which includes italic styles, as well as the Typonine Stencil in regular and condensed forms. The family's city-named styles — Amsterdam through Hong Kong — suggest an international scope suited to editorial and cultural publishing contexts.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Thema Moderato as part of a broader type system that includes stencil and condensed variants, parallel A and B sub-styles across four weights, and a connection to the wider Thema and Typonine Stencil families, with individual styles named after cities across the globe.

name: Thema
classification_notes: Thema presents as a refined text serif with a stencil variant and moderato optical sizes, consistent with a transitional or humanist serif design intended for editorial and text use at multiple sizes.
summary: A serif type system from Typotheque featuring text and moderato optical variants alongside stencil companions, sold as individual named styles.
description: A structured serif system built across four weights with matching italics, a stencil companion, and a Moderato subfamily offering two optical variants per weight. The family is sold as named individual styles — Amsterdam, Bengaluru, Copenhagen and so on — giving each weight and style a distinct identity. The stencil cuts come in both upright and condensed, extending the system's range for display and editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Thema is a type system from Typotheque comprising a core serif family in four weights with italics, a stencil companion called Typonine Stencil in regular, medium and bold with condensed variants, and Thema Moderato with A and B optical variants across the same weight range, also available as a stencil.

name: Tesla Stencil
classification_notes: The stencil construction is the defining feature, placing this firmly in the display category with a stencil subcategory. It is part of the broader Tesla type system, which also includes slab, mono, and caps subfamilies.
summary: A stencil variant within Typotheque's extended Tesla type system, spanning seven weights with matching italics and a variable font option.
description: Part of the Tesla system alongside slab, mono, and caps subfamilies, Tesla Stencil runs from Hairline to Bold with full italic counterparts across each weight. A variable font version is also available, offering fluid control across the weight axis. The system's coherence makes it suited to identities and editorial work where stencil character is needed without sacrificing typographic range.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tesla Stencil as part of the Tesla system, which encompasses stencil, slab, mono, and caps variants. The stencil family offers seven weights from Hairline to Bold, with italics throughout and a variable font option for flexible typographic use.

name: Tesla Slab
classification_notes: Tesla Slab is a slab serif family with consistent stroke weight and geometric slab serifs, positioned within a broader system that includes mono, stencil and caps variants. The even, structured forms align it with contemporary geometric slab traditions.
summary: A structured slab serif family from Typotheque, offered as part of a broader system that includes mono, stencil, caps and variable subfamilies across seven weights.
description: Tesla Slab spans seven weights from Hairline to Bold, each with a matching italic, and belongs to a wider Tesla type system encompassing Tesla Mono, Tesla Caps, Tesla Stencil and a variable font. The consistent geometric construction across subfamilies makes the system well suited to editorial and UI environments where typographic unity across different text roles is required. Named styles follow a city-based naming convention for individual purchases.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tesla Slab as part of the Tesla Slab System, a coordinated family comprising Tesla Slab, Tesla Mono, Tesla Caps, Tesla Stencil and Tesla Slab Variable, offering seven weights with italics and broad stylistic range within a single coherent system.

name: Tesla Mono
classification_notes: Tesla Mono is explicitly a monospaced typeface, part of a larger system including slab, caps and stencil variants, with consistent fixed-width spacing across all weights.
summary: A monospaced type system from Typotheque with seven weights, matching italics, and companion slab, caps and stencil subfamilies.
description: Part of a broader Tesla system, the Mono cut offers seven weights from Hairline to Bold each with a corresponding italic, alongside a variable font version. The family extends into Tesla Slab, Tesla Caps and Tesla Stencil, giving the system considerable range across editorial, technical and display applications. Individual styles are named after cities, a Typotheque convention that reinforces the international character coverage the foundry is known for.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tesla Mono as part of a wider Tesla system that includes slab, caps and stencil variants, available in seven weights with italics and as a variable font. Individual weights are named after world cities from Amsterdam to New Delhi.

name: Tesla Caps
classification_notes: A capitals-only slab serif conceived for headlines and titles, offered in three distinct contrast styles including monolinear, inverted, and high-contrast inverted. Its titling-only nature and strong optical impact place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A capitals-only titling slab from Nikola Djurek, offered in three contrast styles designed to pair with the broader Tesla type system.
description: Designed by Nikola Djurek and released in 2015, Tesla Caps provides three optical variants: a monolinear (A), a slightly inverted contrast (B), and a tightly spaced high-contrast inverted style (C). Each runs across seven weights from Hairline to Bold, with italics throughout. It is conceived as a companion to Tesla Slab, Tesla Mono and Tesla Stencil, forming a complete typographic system suited to demanding editorial and identity work.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Tesla Caps as a capitals-only titling slab with three distinct styles: a thin monolinear, a slightly inverted variant, and a tightly spaced high-contrast inverted cut for strong headlines and titles. It is intended for use alongside the other members of the Tesla type family, which includes Slab, Stencil and Mono versions, offering designers a complete typographic palette.

name: Tempera Biblio
classification_notes: Tempera Biblio is a book-oriented serif system with multiple optical grades (A, B, C) and wide variants designed for long-form text setting, placing it firmly in the book serif tradition.
summary: A multi-grade book serif system from Typotheque, built for demanding long-form text setting with three optical grades and wide variants.
description: Tempera Biblio is a book-focused serif family offering three optical grades (A, B, C) each with italic and wide companions, allowing fine control over colour and density across different paper stocks and print conditions. The system extends into a broader Tempera family with Light through Bold weights and matching italics, giving designers a unified toolkit from body text to display. Its naming after world cities and references to 16th-century Polish book design point to a rigorous historical grounding.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tempera Biblio as a comprehensive book type system with multiple grades and wide variants suited to refined long-form typesetting, complemented by the wider Tempera family spanning Light to Bold with italics throughout.

name: Tempera
classification_notes: Tempera is a text-oriented serif with multiple optical variants (Biblio) and wide cuts suggesting humanist serif construction aimed at book and editorial typography.
summary: A text serif system from Typotheque built across three subfamilies, including optical variants and wide cuts suited to demanding book and editorial typography.
description: Structured around three subfamilies, Tempera covers a standard family with four weights and italics, a Biblio range with three optical variants (Book A, B and C) plus wide counterparts for different typographic conditions, and a variable font. The Biblio cuts suggest careful calibration for small text sizes, a priority more common in typeface systems designed for long-form printed work. The inclusion of wide variants alongside the standard proportions gives designers meaningful control over texture and line density.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Tempera as a system comprising the core family, Tempera Biblio with its optical variants, and a variable font, offering a range of weights, italics and wide cuts for print and editorial applications.

name: Surogat Condensed
classification_notes: The condensed and compressed width variants alongside a standard width, combined with the broad weight range and upright/italic pairings, point to a workhorse grotesque sans-serif system built for demanding typographic environments.
summary: A three-width grotesque system from Typotheque spanning eight weights with matching italics across Condensed, standard and Compressed cuts.
description: The family runs sixteen styles per width, covering Thin through Black with italics throughout, giving designers a wide tonal range within each of the three width variants. The Condensed and Compressed cuts are particularly suited to tightly spaced editorial and display settings where space is at a premium. Individual fonts are sold under city names, from Amsterdam through to Providence, offering granular licensing across the full system.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Surogat Condensed as part of a broader Surogat system that includes standard and Compressed widths, each available in eight weights with corresponding italics, sold individually under city names.

name: Surogat Compressed
classification_notes: Surogat Compressed is a narrow grotesque sans-serif forming part of a wider family that includes upright and condensed widths across an extensive weight range, consistent with the grotesque tradition.
summary: A compressed grotesque sans from Typotheque, part of a three-width family spanning eight weights with italics throughout.
description: Surogat Compressed is the narrowest cut in the Surogat family, which also includes Surogat Condensed and the standard-width Surogat. Eight weights from Thin to Black are available across all three widths, each accompanied by italic styles. The compressed width makes it well suited to tight editorial settings, posters and headlines where horizontal space is at a premium.
foundry_blurb: Surogat Compressed is presented by Typotheque as part of the broader Surogat system, which spans three widths — standard, condensed and compressed — each offered in eight weights from Thin to Black with corresponding italics, providing a flexible range for both text and display applications.

name: Surogat
classification_notes: Surogat presents as a multi-width grotesque sans-serif system, with no visible humanist or geometric tendencies evident from the specimen, structured across three width variants typical of a contemporary editorial grotesque.
summary: A broad grotesque sans system from Typotheque spanning three widths and eight weights, each with matching italics.
description: The family runs across standard, Condensed and Compressed widths, each carrying eight weights from Thin to Black with accompanying italics, giving a total of 48 styles. Individual styles are sold under city names, a naming convention Typotheque uses across the range. The breadth of the system suits editorial and identity work requiring consistent typographic control across different measures and hierarchies.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Surogat as a large type system organised across three width variants — Surogat, Surogat Condensed and Surogat Compressed — each available in eight weights with italics, with individual fonts named after cities around the world.

name: Nota
classification_notes: Nota presents a clean, neutral sans-serif with humanist characteristics, suitable for extended text setting across multiple scripts and languages, consistent with Typotheque's multilingual text type tradition.
summary: A clean humanist sans in four weights with matching italics and a variable font version, designed for extended text work.
description: Nota offers Regular, Medium, Bold and Black weights each with an italic, covering the essentials for editorial and document typography. A variable font version accompanies the static styles. The design sits in familiar humanist sans territory without strong stylistic idiosyncrasies, making it broadly serviceable for text-heavy applications.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque offer Nota as a family of eight static styles spanning four weights with italics, alongside a Nota Variable font. The family is shown set in city names from Amsterdam to Hong Kong, suggesting broad multilingual intent.

name: Mote
classification_notes: Mote presents clean, upright forms across a broad weight range with matching italics, consistent with a humanist sans-serif classification. No strong geometric or grotesque features are evident from the specimen.
summary: A six-weight humanist sans from Typotheque with matching italics and a variable font option.
description: Mote spans Light through Black with corresponding italics, giving twelve static styles in total alongside a variable font for interpolated control. The family suits extended text setting as well as display use across the weight range. Available from Typotheque with per-weight licensing.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque offer Mote as a family of six weights from Light to Black, each with an italic, plus a variable font version. Individual styles are available to purchase separately.

name: Identitet
classification_notes: The family spans Latin, Cyrillic and multiple Glagolitic scripts, suggesting a humanist serif rooted in South Slavic typographic heritage. The breadth of script coverage and named subfamilies point to a scholarly, historically grounded design.
summary: A multi-script family from Typotheque covering Latin, Cyrillic and three distinct Glagolitic variants, rooted in South Slavic typographic and manuscript traditions.
description: Identitet brings together five subfamilies spanning contemporary Latin and Cyrillic alongside three Glagolitic cuts, including a historical 1483 variant and the rounded Glagoljica Obla. The breadth of script coverage is unusual even by Typotheque standards, and the inclusion of Glagolitic places this family in rare company for a commercially available typeface. Available in Regular and Medium, it is aimed at cultural, heritage and academic contexts where script fidelity and historical grounding matter.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque's Identitet is a multi-script family offering five subfamilies across Latin, Cyrillic and Glagolitic scripts, the latter in three forms including a historical 1483 cut and a rounded variant called Glagoljica Obla. The family is available in Regular and Medium weights.

name: Karloff Neutral

classification_notes: Karloff Neutral occupies the midpoint between the high-contrast Didone and the reverse-contrast Italian styles, resulting in a typeface with moderate, balanced contrast that reads as a transitional serif freed from either extreme.
summary: The middle member of a three-part system exploring contrast extremes, Karloff Neutral sits between the high-contrast Didone and the eccentric reverse-contrast Italian, arriving at a composed equilibrium between two irreconcilable extremes.

The Neutral cut comes in a single weight with italic and small caps, supported by a remarkably broad language roster covering Latin, Cyrillic and Greek. Its conceptual premise, resolving two historically opposed contrast models into one coherent form, makes it unusual among text serifs released in the 2010s.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Karloff as an exploration of irreconcilable differences, pairing the high-contrast Didone, widely regarded as among the most beautiful typefaces, with the eccentric reverse-contrast Italian, historically one of typography's most provocative styles. Karloff Neutral represents the midpoint of this three-way system, available in a single weight with italic and small caps.

Three weights run from Regular to Bold, each with a matching italic, and named style bundles such as Amsterdam, Bengaluru and Copenhagen allow individual purchase of specific weight-style combinations.

summary: A conceptual display typeface by Peter Biľak in which each letter is formed by a human body, delivered as an OpenType SBIX colour bitmap font.

Coverage is limited to the basic Latin alphabet with no numerals, punctuation or diacritics, a constraint the foundry attributes to the natural limits of the human body.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque describe Body Type as a functional type system built from human bodies, with Peter Biľak drawing on his experience with dance and movement installation work. The family includes Regular and Bold weights covering the 26-letter Latin alphabet, with contributors Valentina Scaglia and Lisa van Bommel assisting in Latin character development.

The counters of Klimax Plus mirror the stroke weight of Klimax Minus, and the italic cuts feature curved terminals with calligraphic character. Small caps for Plus are pushed to geometric extremes, eliminating inner negative space while retaining legibility in most contexts.

name: Brioni
classification_notes: Brioni is a humanist serif with a clear text-oriented design system, offered in two optical sizes (display and text), suggesting careful attention to reading performance across sizes typical of humanist serif traditions.
summary: A humanist serif system from Typotheque split across two optical sizes, covering six weights with italics throughout.
description: The family divides into Brioni for display settings and Brioni Text for body copy, each running from Thin to Bold with matching italics. The two-size system reflects a considered approach to optical scaling rather than simply scaling a single master. Well-suited to editorial and publishing work where visual consistency across headlines and running text is required.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Brioni as a system comprising a display family and a text family, each offering six weights from Thin to Bold with corresponding italics, designed to cover a range of editorial and typographic applications.

name: History
classification_notes: History is a modular, layered type system composed of 21 discrete layers that can be combined to produce historically-referencing composite letterforms; it resists a single traditional classification and sits firmly in the display category as a chromatic or layered type system.
summary: A 21-layer modular type system from Typotheque that lets designers stack discrete letterform components to construct historically-inflected chromatic display type.
description: History is built from 21 numbered layers, each encoding a different structural or decorative element drawn from type history, which can be combined freely to produce compound letterforms with depth, texture and chromatic complexity. Pre-set combinations are named after cities, from Amsterdam to Zaragoza, offering starting points that demonstrate the breadth of the system. It is one of the most architecturally rigorous layered typefaces in contemporary type design, with a combinatorial range that goes well beyond typical two-colour chromatic fonts.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present History as a layered type family built from 21 individual fonts, each representing a distinct historical layer or decorative element. These layers can be combined in any configuration, with Typotheque providing a series of named city presets — from Amsterdam to Zaragoza — to illustrate possible combinations.

name: Marlene
classification_notes: Marlene presents as a text serif with upright and italic cuts across multiple weights, and includes a stencil variant and a Grande optical size, pointing to a classical serif tradition with contemporary application range.
summary: A serif type system from Typotheque offered in three subfamilies: a standard text cut, a stencil variant and a Grande optical size, each in four weights.
description: The system spans three distinct subfamilies: Marlene for text setting with italics, Marlene Stencil as an upright-only variant, and Marlene Grande for larger optical applications. All three share a four-weight range from Light to Bold, giving the system coherent tonal flexibility across size and context. Named city editions suggest multilingual or regional licensing options, pointing to broad international application.
foundry_blurb: Typotheque present Marlene as a system with three subfamilies including a stencil cut and a Grande optical size, available across Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights with matching italics for the text and Grande variants. Individual city-named licences are available for purchase.
